message:
fix: resolve React hydration mismatches and improve browser compatibility

This commit addresses SSR hydration issues and improves cross-browser support:

1. stats-grid.tsx - AnimatedNumber hydration fix:
   - Initialize state with SSR-safe values (0/false) instead of using
     prefersReducedMotion which differs between server and client
   - Handle reduced motion preference after hydration via useEffect
   - Eliminates "Text content did not match" React hydration errors

2. use-mobile-optimizations.ts - SSR safety and cleanup improvements:
   - Add defaultCapabilities constant for SSR-safe initial render
   - Track hydration state with isHydrated flag
   - Properly clear orientation change timeouts on unmount
   - Add Safari < 14 fallback for mediaQuery listeners (addListener/removeListener)
   - Expose isHydrated in return value for components that need to know

3. use-reduced-motion.ts - Safari compatibility:
   - Add fallback for Safari < 14 which doesn't support addEventListener on
     MediaQueryList (uses deprecated addListener/removeListener instead)

These fixes ensure the site renders correctly without hydration warnings
across all supported browsers including older Safari versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
